LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS THE SF EXAMINER, TODD VOGT, MIKE ALDAX, AND ARI BURACK'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, AS TO THE 3RD, 5TH, 6TH AND 7TH CAUSES OF ACTION AND OVERRULED AS TO THE 1ST, 2ND AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR GENERAL NEGLIGENCE, VIOLATION OF THE RIGHT TO PUBLICITY, INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTION DISTRESS AND NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. FOR THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED THE ELEMENTS TO ALLEGE GENERAL NEGLIGENCE, AND THERE IS NO COGNIZABLE CLAIM FOR FAILURE TO HAVE DEFAMATORY ARTICLES RETRACTED AND/OR DELETED FROM INTERNET MEDIUM. FOR THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF RIGHT OF PUBLICITY, PLAINTIFF REQUESTS LEAVE TO AMEND IN HIS OPPOSITION TO THE DEMURRER, AND LEAVE TO AMEND WILL BE GRANTED. FOR THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED THAT DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT WAS OUTRAGEOUS. (SEE JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA CIVIL JURY INSTRUCTIONS ("CACI") JURY INSTRUCTION NUMBER 1600(1).) FOR THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED THAT DEFENDANT'S NEGLIGENCE WAS A SUBSTANTIAL FACTOR IN CAUSING PLAINTIFF' SERIOUS EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. (SEE CACI JURY INSTRUCTION NUMBER 1620(3).) THE 1ST, 2ND AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE ADEQUATELY PLED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS THE SF EXAMINER, TODD VOGT, MIKE ALDAX, and ARI BURACK'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS Sustained, with 20 days leave to amend, as to the 3rd, 5th, 6th and 7th causes of action and overruled as to the 1st, 2nd and 4th causes of action. Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege causes of action for general negligence, violation of the right to publicity, intentional infliction of emotion distress and negligent infliction of emotional distress. For the 3rd cause of action, Plaintiff has not pled the elements to allege general negligence, and there is no cognizable claim for failure to have defamatory articles retracted and/or deleted from internet medium. For the 5th cause of action for violation of right of publicity, Plaintiff requests leave to amend in his opposition to the demurrer, and leave to amend will be granted. For the 6th cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress, Plaintiff has not pled that Defendant's conduct was outrageous. (See Judicial Council of California Civil Jury Instructions ("CACI") Jury Instruction number 1600(1).) For the 7th cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress, Plaintiff has not pled that Defendant's negligence was a substantial factor in causing Plaintiff' serious emotional distress. (See CACI Jury Instruction number 1620(3).) The 1st, 2nd and 4th causes of action are adequately pled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS THE SF EXAMINER, TODD VOGT, MIKE ALDAX, and ARI BURACK'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS Denied. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to support a claim for punitive damages. (Civ. Code 3294(a). (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185
